message:
feat(core): optimize autocommit paths for in-memory connections

Skip external pager publication refresh for :memory: connections since
they are process-local and cannot be invalidated by an external pager
instance — the published plane is already authoritative.

Introduce `resolve_autocommit_txn_with_capture` to control whether
successful autocommit writes retain a time-travel snapshot.  Ordinary
DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) now skips the expensive MemDatabase clone
on every statement, while DDL and other structural changes continue to
capture snapshots for `FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF` queries.

Add `pager_publication_refreshes` counter to the hot-path profiling
infrastructure so the optimisation is observable in benchmarks.

Two new tests verify that autocommit :memory: inserts avoid file-backed
publication refreshes and that autocommit DML does not accumulate
time-travel snapshots.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
